studyName: Structure and Diversity of Urinary Cell-Free DNA Informative of Host-Pathogen Interactions in Human Urinary Tract Infection

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, sample collection day after transplantation, infection status, sample collection method, bacterial culture, bacteria identification in urine, qPCR for BK polyomavirus, number of BK polyomavirus fragments determined by qPCR, sequencing center, platform and read lengths, body site where sample was collected, analyte type, tumor status, subject category, sample type based on fluid from which DNA was extracted, and IBIS platform to identify bacteria.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BACTERIAL_CULTURE
variables.description: Was a bacterial culture performed on the sample?

variables.name: BK_QPCR
variables.description: Was a qPCR assay for BK polyomavirus genes performed?

variables.name: BK_QPCR_COPYNUMBER
variables.description: Number of BK polyomavirus fragments determined by qPCR assay.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: COLLECTION_DAY
variables.description: Day after transplantation on which urine sample was collected (negative numbers refer to days before transplant)

variables.name: COLLECTION_METHOD
variables.description: Method by which clinicians collected patient sample

variables.name: IBIS_BACTERIAL_USED
variables.description: Was IBIS platform used to identify bacteria

variables.name: IDENTIFICATION
variables.description: Bacteria determined >10,000 CFU in urine culture.

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_TYPE
variables.description: Describes fluid from which DNA was extracted

variables.name: SEQUENCING_CENTER
variables.description: Location where sequencing was performed

variables.name: SEQUENCING_PLATFORM
variables.description: Brand and model of next-generation sequencing platform

variables.name: SEQUENCING_READ_LENGTHS
variables.description: Description of length of raw-data reads

variables.name: STATUS
variables.description: Infection status

variables.name: SUBJECT_TYPE
variables.description: Characterizes subject

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, donor sex, recipient sex, fetal sex, broad sample category based on infection status, narrow sample cateogy based on infection diagnosis, and case control status of the subject for opportunistic infections.

variables.name: CLINICAL_DIAGNOSIS
variables.description: Narrow sample cateogy based on infection diagnosis of clinician

variables.name: CONDITION
variables.description: Condition of the subject

variables.name: DONOR_SEX
variables.description: Sex of patient donating kidney in the case of renal transplant

variables.name: FETAL_SEX
variables.description: Sex of fetus in the case of pregnancy

variables.name: MeSH_PHENOTYPE
variables.description: Broad sample category based on infection status

variables.name: RECIPIENT_SEX
variables.description: Sex of patient receiving kidney  in the case of renal transplant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified patient identification number

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, subject source, and sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Conseny group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Patient sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Patient Identification number

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
